body_txt: When a character decides that she will have a baby, by any means necessary. Many women dream of having their own children someday. Unfortunately, that dream doesn't always come true. Whether through accident, trauma, genetic incompatibility, or just plain bad luck, some women don't conceive despite their best efforts. Some suffer Tragic Stillbirth or a not-so- Convenient Miscarriage . A handful have managed to pull off a Fake Pregnancy so well that everyone thinks Baby could arrive any day now. And a few just envy what others have. Whatever the reason, these women believe that they NEED a baby ... and they'll do whatever it takes to get one. Ways of achieving this include: As having one's child stolen is any parent's nightmare , characters willing to take someone else's baby are almost never portrayed sympathetically. Common motivations include: The Baby Trap , Darwinist Desire , or Mandatory Motherhood . It can involve a Breeding Slave or a Stalker with a Test Tube . The common Freudian Excuse for this is a miscarriage or a Tragic Stillbirth . The woman's fixation on having a child often epitomizes Babies Make Everything Better . Compare Kill the Parent, Raise the Child , where a character adopts and raises a slain enemy's offspring, which can overlap with this trope if the child-seeker is determined enough to have someone else's children to kill for them. Also related to Pregnancy Makes You Crazy and the Law of Inverse Fertility , in that the couples that are the most desperate for a baby are the least likely to be able to have one. May cross over with The Kindnapper if the kidnapper believes that would be a better parent for the baby than the actual parents. Not to be confused with I'm Taking Her Home with Me! , which is more about finding someone else particularly cute and lovable.
